boards.name: Espressif ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 N16R8
boards.notes: Native USB CDC. Use the USB port (not UART). Hold BOOT during reset if it doesn't enter bootloader mode.

boards.name: Waveshare ESP32-S3-GEEK (1.14" ST7789)
boards.notes: Native USB CDC. Mining + AxeHub HTTP API enabled; built-in 1.14" 240x135 ST7789 uses upstream display (the AxeHub 3-screen layout is CYD-only for now).

boards.name: Sunton ESP32-2432S024 (CYD 2.4")
boards.notes: ILI9341_2 driver, TFT_BL=GPIO27, capacitive touch (CST816S, not yet supported).

boards.name: M5Stack M5StickC Plus 2 (1.14" ST7789v2)
boards.notes: ESP32-PICO-V3-02 with built-in 1.14" 240x135 ST7789v2, battery, 2 buttons. Mining + AxeHub HTTP API + pipelined HW SHA enabled; built-in display uses upstream M5 driver (the AxeHub 3-screen layout is CYD-only for now).

boards.name: AI-Thinker ESP32-CAM
boards.notes: Requires ESP32-CAM-MB programmer (or external FTDI + manual GPIO0→GND). KNOWN ISSUE: web flashing typically fails with 'No serial data received' because esptool-js (Chrome WebSerial) has a ~3s timeout for the flash_begin ACK, but the stub needs 5–10s to erase 1.3 MB of flash sectors. PIO esptool works (longer timeout + retry). Workaround: use `pio run -e esp32cam --target upload --upload-port COMx` from command line.
